decorator-quantifies-composition-advanta.text: Decorator's precise exponential advantage (N classes vs 2^N) for composing orthogonal behaviors, combined with its simultaneous support for SRP (each decorator = one concern) and OCP (extend without modify), provides a quantitative grounding for the qualitative composition-over-inheritance principle — the combinatorial explosion is the measurable cost that composition avoids.

justifications.label: Decorator gives composition-over-inheritance its most concrete quantitative justification

decorator-solves-subclass-explosion.text: The Decorator pattern solves exponential subclass proliferation by composing behavior at runtime instead of through inheritance

decorator-strictly-superior-to-subclassi.text: Decorator is strictly superior to subclassing for combining orthogonal behaviors, requiring N classes vs 2^N and operating per-object at runtime rather than per-class at compile time.

justifications.label: Decorator's combinatorial advantage holds only if forwarding boilerplate is acceptable or mitigated

decorator-supports-srp-and-ocp.text: The Decorator pattern supports both the Single Responsibility Principle (each decorator encapsulates one concern) and the Open-Closed Principle (extends behavior without modifying existing code)

delegation-and-structural-subtyping-conv.text: The composition infrastructure evolves at two independent levels — delegation's concept persists while its mechanisms evolve across paradigms (concept-mechanism decoupling), and structural subtyping converges across languages replacing nominal inheritance — together pointing toward a post-inheritance paradigm where composition requires neither explicit inheritance declarations nor paradigm-specific delegation patterns.

justifications.label: two independent composition evolution trajectories (mechanism-level decoupling and type-level convergence) independently eliminate different inheritance dependencies

delegation-can-always-replace-inheritanc.text: According to the GoF book, delegation can always replace inheritance, but adds runtime complexity

delegation-concept-will-outlive-delegati.text: Delegation is simultaneously the current universal mechanism for composition across both class-based and prototype-based paradigms AND subject to concept-mechanism decoupling — the composition concept that delegation serves will persist even as delegation's specific mechanisms (forwarding references, proxy objects, prototype chains, CRTP wrappers) evolve or dissolve into language features, just as the mixin concept outlived Flavors/CLOS/CRTP and the notification concept outlived java.util.Observer.

justifications.label: Concept-mechanism decoupling applies reflexively to delegation itself — the universality of the mechanism does not guarantee its permanence

delegation-is-universal-composition-mech.text: Delegation is the universal runtime mechanism for composition across both class-based and prototype-based paradigms, and also the unifying mechanism behind all four structural wrapper patterns, making it the single implementation primitive that realizes the GoF's central organizing axis.

justifications.label: Delegation bridges paradigms AND unifies wrapper patterns — it is the shared primitive underlying composition-over-inheritance at both the paradigm level and the pattern level

delegation-per-instance-runtime-inherita.text: Delegation operates per-instance at runtime, while inheritance typically operates per-type at compile time

delegation-persistence-cleanly-self-corr.text: Delegation's predicted persistence at the dissolution fixed point — structurally predicted by concept-mechanism decoupling and dynamically stabilized by three-scale self-correction at the dissolving-enduring boundary — achieves clean concept-level survival without residual identity problems, with self-correction smoothly managing delegation's transition across paradigms.

justifications.label: delegation survival is both structurally predicted and dynamically stabilized, but object schizophrenia could represent a concept-level defect (not just mechanism-level) that self-correction cannot resolve — if identity ambiguity is intrinsic to delegation rather than to specific delegation mechanisms, the predicted clean survival is compromised

delegation-persistence-predicted-by-diss.text: Delegation's concept-mechanism decoupling (the delegation concept persists while specific delegation mechanisms evolve across paradigms) is structurally predicted by the dissolution fixed point at the module framework: delegation addresses inter-module communication and composition — concerns orthogonal to the type system — so it persists at the boundary between dissolving and enduring OO knowledge, confirming the fixed point's explanatory power at the individual-concept level.

justifications.label: delegation is a module-framework concern orthogonal to the type system, so the fixed-point theory predicts its persistence — and concept-mechanism decoupling confirms it empirically

delegation-primary-reuse-in-prototype-la.text: Delegation is the fundamental method for behavior reuse in prototype-based programming (Self, JavaScript), analogous to inheritance in class-based programming

delegation-self-rebinding-explains-schiz.text: The object schizophrenia problem is structurally determined by delegation's self-rebinding semantics: because delegation (unlike forwarding) rebinds this/self to the original sender, a delegated method operates with ambiguous identity — the receiver's state but the sender's type — creating the exact identity confusion that schizophrenia describes.

justifications.label: Delegation's definitional self-rebinding is the structural mechanism that produces the schizophrenia phenomenon

delegation-transcends-class-prototype-pa.text: Delegation serves as the universal behavioral mechanism across both class-based OO (underpinning composition patterns: Bridge delegates to implementors, Proxy to real subjects, State/Strategy to behavioral variants) and prototype-based OO (serving as the fundamental reuse mechanism replacing class inheritance via cloning) — it is the single mechanism that operates identically across the class/prototype paradigm divide.

justifications.label: Delegation operates as the universal mechanism in both class-based and prototype-based OO paradigms

delegation-underpins-composition-pattern.text: Delegation is the runtime mechanism underlying composition-based GoF patterns: Bridge delegates to implementors, Proxy delegates to real subjects, and delegation can always replace inheritance.

justifications.label: composition patterns share delegation as their common runtime mechanism

delegation-universal-but-identity-fragil.text: Delegation provides the universal runtime mechanism for composition-over-inheritance across all structural wrapper patterns, unified by LoD enforcement at module boundaries.

justifications.label: depth-3 gated — delegation unifies composition patterns universally, but object schizophrenia (self-reference ambiguity under delegation chains) undermines identity guarantees

delegation-vs-forwarding-self-rebinding.text: Delegation rebinds self/this to the original sender object, while forwarding does not — self in the receiver's method refers to the receiver itself in forwarding

dependency-injection-is-not-ioc.text: Dependency injection is not the same as Inversion of Control — DI is one specific pattern for dependency resolution often associated with IoC containers, but IoC originally refers to control flow inversion

dependency-injection-passes-dependencies.text: Dependency injection means needed objects are passed in rather than created internally, improving testability and decoupling.

design-patterns-originated-christopher-a.text: Design patterns originated from Christopher Alexander's architectural pattern language concept (1977), adapted to software by Kent Beck and Ward Cunningham at OOPSLA 1987

di-replaces-singleton-as-global-access-m.text: Dependency injection provides the same shared-instance access capability as Singleton without its anti-pattern drawbacks (global state, hidden coupling, testing difficulty) — a replacement the GoF authors themselves validated by listing DI among the patterns they would retroactively add to the catalog.

justifications.label: GoF's own retrospective endorsement of DI confirms it as Singleton's successor for managed global access

dip-abstractions-owned-by-high-level-lay.text: In DIP, the abstractions (interfaces) are owned by the higher/policy layer, not the lower implementation layer.

dip-acyclicity-exemplifies-solid-archite.text: DIP's enforcement of acyclic dependency structure — both high-level and low-level modules depending on shared abstractions rather than on each other — is a concrete instance of SOLID's external module quality governance operating at the architectural level: the abstraction-mediated dependency rule translates SOLID's abstract coupling constraint into a verifiable structural property (directed acyclic graph), demonstrating that SOLID principles yield architectural guarantees beyond class-level design guidance.

justifications.label: DIP's acyclicity requirement is SOLID external governance made structurally verifiable — the abstract principle becomes a graph property

dip-and-di-complementary-not-identical.text: DIP (design principle specifying dependency direction via abstractions owned by the high-level layer) and DI (runtime mechanism passing dependencies into consumers) are complementary but non-identical: DIP defines WHAT the dependency structure should look like, DI provides HOW to achieve it at runtime — and each can hold independently of the other.

justifications.label: Design principle and runtime mechanism address complementary concerns and are independently applicable

dip-depend-on-abstractions.text: The Dependency Inversion Principle states that high-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions rather than concretions.

dip-differs-from-dependency-injection.text: DIP is a design principle specifying dependency structure; Dependency Injection is a runtime mechanism for providing implementations that satisfy DIP abstractions.

dip-effective-when-abstractions-thoughtf.text: DIP effectively inverts dependency direction when abstractions are thoughtfully designed — owned by the high-level policy layer and implemented through deliberate strategy choices (direct or flexible) — producing genuine architectural decoupling.

justifications.label: Mechanical interface wrapping without thoughtful abstraction design fails to achieve DIP's coupling reduction goals

dip-enforces-acyclic-dependency-structur.text: DIP's ownership rule — abstractions owned by the high-level policy layer, with both high and low-level modules depending on those abstractions rather than on each other — supports acyclic dependencies: when dependency arrows point toward the abstraction layer rather than between concrete modules, cycles are less likely to form.

justifications.label: DIP's abstraction-ownership rule structurally prevents dependency cycles by eliminating direct inter-module references

dip-is-d-in-solid.text: The Dependency Inversion Principle is the 'D' in SOLID, one of five OO design principles.

dip-not-low-depends-on-high.text: DIP does not mean low-level modules depend on high-level modules; it means both depend on a shared abstraction layer.

dip-originated-by-robert-c-martin.text: Robert C. Martin originated the Dependency Inversion Principle, first in a 1994 paper and later in a 1996 C++ Report article.

dip-statement-one-both-depend-on-abstrac.text: DIP statement 1: High-level modules should not import from low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions.

dip-statement-two-abstractions-not-depen.text: DIP statement 2: Abstractions should not depend on details; details should depend on abstractions.

dip-two-implementation-strategies.text: DIP has two implementation strategies: direct (abstractions co-packaged with policy layer, inverting compilation dependency) and separated (abstractions in independent packages, maximizing reuse).

dip-two-statements-complete-dependency-s.text: DIP's two statements address complementary aspects of dependency management: statement 1 (both high-level and low-level modules depend on abstractions) aims to eliminate vertical coupling between layers, while statement 2 (abstractions don't depend on details) aims to keep the abstraction layer itself stable. Together they target the major dependency leakage paths between layers and their implementations.

justifications.label: The two statements address orthogonal dependency directions (inter-layer and abstraction-to-detail) forming a complete specification

dip-wrapping-every-class-in-interface-no.text: Wrapping every class in an interface does not automatically reduce coupling; only thoughtful abstraction of interactions achieves that.

dispatch-cluster-dissolution-qualified-b.text: Single dispatch's poverty fully explains the GoF's most complex cluster (Visitor+Composite+Iterator) and that cluster fully dissolves in languages with richer dispatch or sum types — but the dissolved solution achieves clean extensibility only if behavioral subtyping at the new extension points is decidable, which it is not in general, leaving a residual verification gap even after the pattern machinery is absorbed.

justifications.label: Dispatch cluster dissolves mechanically but behavioral verification gap persists at extension points

dispatch-hierarchy-orthogonal-to-polymor.text: The three forms of polymorphism (ad hoc, parametric, subtyping) characterize a type system's expressiveness, while the dispatch hierarchy (single → double → multiple) characterizes runtime method-resolution capability — these appear to be orthogonal dimensions, since the antecedents define them independently without coupling one to the other.

justifications.label: Polymorphism and dispatch are independent type-system axes whose intersection generates pattern complexity

dispatch-poverty-and-inheritance-error-a.text: Single dispatch poverty (generating complexity compensable by type-system enrichment) and inheritance's type-system design error (generating a compensatory ecosystem of SOLID constraints and composition patterns) are two independent dissolution engines. Dispatch poverty drives dissolution of dispatch-dependent patterns while inheritance error drives dissolution of patterns compensating for inheritance pathologies. Their independence suggests that dissolution of many GoF patterns may be overdetermined, with multiple sufficient causes operating in parallel.

justifications.label: Two independent dissolution engines (dispatch poverty, inheritance error) jointly explain complete catalog dissolution

dispatch-poverty-explains-entire-visitor.text: The Visitor pattern's double-dispatch mechanism — a key source of complexity in GoF behavioral patterns — is primarily an artifact of single-dispatch limitations: the expression problem at its core is structurally determined by dispatch deficiencies, and double dispatch dissolves via two independent language paths (multiple dispatch and dynamic typing), indicating that this complexity is substantially reducible in languages with richer dispatch mechanisms.

justifications.label: Both the expression problem (at Composite) and double dispatch (at Visitor) are single-dispatch artifacts; their cluster complexity is therefore entirely dispatch-determined

dispatch-poverty-fully-compensable-by-ty.text: Both the dispatch mechanism workaround (double dispatch dissolves via multiple dispatch languages AND dynamic typing) and the extensibility barrier it generates (expression problem dissolves with sum types and pattern matching) independently dissolve with richer type systems, confirming that single-dispatch poverty is entirely compensable by type-system enrichment with no residual design debt.

justifications.label: Both the mechanism and the barrier from dispatch poverty dissolve independently

dissolution-cascades-follow-pattern-stru.text: Interpreter's dissolution dependency on Composite (its AST is a Composite instance whose type-system tensions drive Interpreter's limitations), combined with Composite's role as the structural-behavioral nexus that validates mechanism-before-governance dissolution ordering, provides evidence that pattern dissolution cascades can follow the same structural dependency relationships as the patterns themselves — at least within the behavioral mechanism cluster anchored by Composite.

justifications.label: Interpreter's Composite dependency creating dissolution cascade proves dissolution follows structural dependency chains

dissolution-communication-substrate-reli.text: The dissolution trajectory's convergence on pub/sub as the enduring inter-module communication substrate — predicted by Observer's language internalization trajectory and validated by the module framework's persistence beyond pattern dissolution — delivers reliable architectural communication infrastructure unless pub/sub's lack of guaranteed delivery by default undermines the reliability of the very communication substrate that the enduring module framework depends on for its external coordination dimension.

justifications.label: The module framework persists and its communication dimension evolves from Observer to pub/sub, but if the evolved substrate cannot guarantee delivery, the framework's communication pillar rests on an unreliable foundation

dissolution-confirmed-by-internal-reflex.text: The dissolving partition's triple reflexivity (self-aware, predictive, enacting — confirmed from structural analysis alone) combined with the dissolution trajectory's empirically confirmed triple properties (causal inevitability, temporal ordering, knowledge preservation — confirmed by actual language evolution) demonstrates that the OO knowledge system's dissolution is overdetermined by two independent epistemic modes: internal structural analysis and external empirical observation converge on the same conclusion.

justifications.label: Internal structural analysis and external empirical observation independently confirm dissolution

dissolution-converges-on-type-system-the.text: The GoF catalog's total dissolution trajectory — including its most fundamental structural tension at Composite — combined with the identification of extensibility concerns as type system limitations rather than inherent design challenges, suggests that a significant portion of classical OO design knowledge addresses problems that are better understood as type system gaps: dissolving patterns mark cases where a language has internalized capabilities that the pattern was working around.

justifications.label: Total dissolution plus extensibility-as-type-limitation implies convergence toward type theory as the terminal destination

dissolution-fixed-point-analytically-pre.text: The dissolution's fixed point at the module framework (orthogonal to the type system) provides a precise analytical boundary between what dissolves and what endures — unless the module framework's core measurement tool (cohesion) is fundamentally ordinal rather than quantitative, in which case the boundary is identifiable but the enduring framework lacks the analytical precision to fully replace the quantitative guarantees that the dissolved governance system aspired to provide.

justifications.label: Dissolution boundary is locatable but the surviving framework's measurement precision may be inherently limited

dissolution-fixed-point-is-scale-invaria.text: The module framework's persistence at the dissolution fixed point and the criterion of scale-invariance are closely related properties: the fixed point consists of concepts that are orthogonal to the type system, and scale-invariance across architectural levels offers a primary explanation for why those concepts resist type-system-driven dissolution — suggesting that orthogonality to the type system and scale-invariance are two perspectives on the same underlying resilience, rather than provably identical properties.

justifications.label: The fixed point and the persistence criterion converge: scale-invariance simultaneously defines what endures and why

dissolution-fixed-point-precisely-measur.text: The dissolution fixed point at the module framework provides a precise analytical boundary between dissolving and enduring OO knowledge, enabling quantitative characterization of what fraction of design knowledge is permanent — but only if the enduring module framework's quality metrics are themselves precise rather than merely ordinal.

justifications.label: The fixed point boundary is analytically sharp, but measurement precision depends on whether cohesion achieves interval-scale measurement

dissolution-fixed-point-predicts-specifi.text: The dissolution fixed point's explanatory power extends beyond the general prediction that the module framework persists to predicting specific concept survivals: delegation persists via concept-mechanism decoupling (the delegation concept outlives its paradigm-specific mechanisms), while encapsulation persists as a complete vertical stack (principle-mechanism-pattern hierarchy orthogonal to the type system), and both survival paths are structurally predicted by the module framework's location at the fixed point where type-system dependence reaches zero.

justifications.label: two specific concept survivals (delegation via decoupling, encapsulation via vertical stack) arrive at the fixed point from independent paths, confirming the fixed point's predictive specificity beyond general framework persistence

dissolution-has-fixed-point-at-module-fr.text: The ordered dissolution of OO design (mechanisms first, then governance-pathology pairs) has a natural fixed point: the module framework persists because it addresses concerns orthogonal to the type system — dissolution propagates through everything type-system-dependent but cannot cross the orthogonality boundary, establishing the module framework as the terminus where the ordered dissolution sequence halts.

justifications.label: Ordered dissolution halts at the module framework because type-system orthogonality is an impassable boundary

dissolution-inevitable-ordered-and-knowl.text: The dissolution trajectory is simultaneously causally inevitable (quadruply determined by four independent forces — two internal poverty sources and two external confirmations), directionally constrained (Composite's structural-behavioral nexus validating the mechanism-before-governance ordering), and correctively managed (three-scale self-correction — concept-level decoupling, catalog-level self-healing, system-level boundary identification — preserving knowledge through the transition), establishing that dissolution is a fully characterized convergent process rather than an open-ended trajectory.

justifications.label: three independent depth-9 conclusions provide causal (why), directional (in what order), and corrective (preserving what) characterization of the same dissolution process

dissolution-ordered-mechanisms-before-go.text: OO design dissolution follows a temporally ordered sequence: mechanisms dissolve first (open recursion yields to composition-based alternatives), then governance frameworks co-dissolve with the pathologies they manage (SOLID becomes unnecessary as inheritance pathologies disappear) — a layered unwinding, not simultaneous collapse.

justifications.label: Mechanism-first dissolution and governance-pathology co-dissolution together reveal a phased ordering: the mechanisms that embody patterns go first, then the principles that constrained them depart with their target pathologies

dissolution-overdetermined-by-internal-a.text: The GoF catalog's dissolution trajectory is overdetermined — confirmed independently by internal forces (the catalog follows its own composition-over-inheritance organizing axis toward dissolution) and external forces (AOP demonstrates that 17/23 patterns' code-level dependencies can be removed) — establishing that dissolution does not depend on any single evolutionary force and would proceed even if one confirmation path were blocked.

justifications.label: Internal (self-organizing axis) and external (AOP) confirmations are independent, making dissolution overdetermined rather than contingent on either alone

dissolution-quadruply-determined.text: OO pattern dissolution is confirmed by four independent forces: two internal poverty sources (single dispatch generating mechanism complexity via the Visitor+Composite+Iterator cluster, inheritance error generating governance complexity via the SOLID+GoF compensatory ecosystem) and two validating dimensions (the catalog's internal self-organization along its own composition axis, external AOP validation removing code-level dependencies from 17/23 patterns).

justifications.label: Two poverty sources × two confirming dimensions yield four independent lines of evidence for the same dissolution trajectory

dissolution-quintuply-determined-includi.text: OO pattern dissolution is supported by five identified forces: the four previously identified (two internal poverty sources generating complementary dissolution arcs, two external validation dimensions) plus the structural instability within OOP's own foundational pillar framework (two of three pillars antagonistic, inheritance-subtyping gap theoretically unbridgeable). The pillar instability represents a conceptually distinct force because it operates at the framework-definition level — concerning the coherence of OOP's self-description rather than its language mechanisms or external competitive pressures — though its practical effects may overlap with the internal poverty forces it partially underlies.

justifications.label: The four existing dissolution forces operate at the language/pattern level; pillar instability operates at the conceptual/philosophical level, making it a genuinely independent fifth confirmatory force

dissolution-trajectory-properties-empiri.text: The dissolution trajectory's characterization as causally inevitable (quadruply determined), directionally constrained (mechanism-before-governance ordering), and knowledge-preserving (three-scale self-correction) receives partial empirical support from actual language evolution — modern languages have internalized composition, reduced inheritance dependence, and dissolved patterns into type system features along predicted pathways. This suggests dissolution is not merely a theoretical prediction but a process with observable real-world correlates, though the degree to which these language changes confirm the specific causal mechanisms (rather than merely being consistent with them) remains a qualified rather than conclusive correspondence.

justifications.label: Empirical language evolution independently confirms theoretically derived dissolution properties

dissolution-trajectory-total-no-permanen.text: The GoF catalog's dissolution trajectory encompasses even its most fundamental structural tension — the expression problem at Composite — confirming that no pattern-level challenge in the catalog is immune to language evolution and that all GoF structural tensions are contingent rather than permanent.

justifications.label: Even the hardest completeness barrier dissolves with richer type systems, making dissolution total

dissolving-partition-constituted-and-sta.text: The dissolving partition of OO knowledge is constituted by its content (inheritance error compensation generating the SOLID+GoF ecosystem) and plausibly supported at its boundary by three-scale self-correction — but these two observations remain independently established rather than demonstrated as coupled mechanisms, so the partition's robustness may derive primarily from the content-level explanation (type-system dependency) rather than from proven dual-mechanism reinforcement.

justifications.label: The partition's interior (what fills it) and its boundary (what maintains its edge) are independently determined

dissolving-partition-reflexive-aware-pre.text: The dissolving partition of OO knowledge is reflexive at three independent levels: self-aware (constituted by inheritance error compensation that recognizes its own contingency), predictive (the dissolution fixed point predicts specific concept survivals down to delegation and encapsulation forms), and self-enacting (three-scale self-correction stabilizes the dissolving-enduring boundary through the very process of dissolution) — a uniquely self-describing epistemic structure.

justifications.label: Three independent reflexive properties emerge from combining constitution/stabilization with predictive specificity

metadata.retract_reason: repair: abandoned — At depth 12 with a flagged ancestor, this belief layers interpretive reframings ('self-aware', 'self-enacting') that attribute awareness and agency to an epistemic structure — claims neither antecedent makes. The 'three independent reflexive levels' framing is the claim's own contribution, not a derivation. Softening would require stripping so much that nothing distinctive remains, and searching for additional antecedents won't help because the core problem is anthropomorphizing a structural analysis. The underlying observations about constitution/stabilization and predictive specificity are already captured by the antecedent beliefs without the reflexivity overlay.

django-mtv-renames-mvc-components.text: Django uses an MTV variant of MVC where 'view' means controller and 'template' means view.

double-dispatch-callback-mechanism.text: Double dispatch works via two sequential single-dispatch calls: the receiver dispatches on its own type, then calls back into the argument passing this/self, causing a second vtable lookup on the argument's concrete type

double-dispatch-callback-reveals-single-.text: Double dispatch's mechanism — two sequential single-dispatch calls where the receiver dispatches on its own type then calls back with type information — explicitly reveals how single-dispatch poverty generates complexity: each invocation resolves only one runtime type, requiring the callback choreography to resolve a second, confirming that the Visitor pattern's structural complexity is entirely mechanical compensation.

justifications.label: Callback choreography mechanistically explains why single-dispatch limitation produces Visitor's structural overhead

double-dispatch-dissolves-via-two-indepe.text: The Visitor/double-dispatch pattern dissolves through two independent language evolution paths: multiple dispatch (CLOS, Julia, Dylan make double dispatch native) and dynamic runtime resolution (C# dynamic keyword triggers runtime overload resolution) — confirming from independent directions that the pattern is a static type system workaround.

justifications.label: Two independent dissolution mechanisms (native multiple dispatch and dynamic runtime resolution) confirm the pattern's contingency from different type system evolution paths

double-dispatch-special-case-of-multiple.text: Double dispatch is the N=2 special case of multiple dispatch; languages with native multiple dispatch (CLOS, Julia, Dylan) do not need the double dispatch workaround

double-dispatch-two-runtime-types.text: Double dispatch selects which function to invoke based on the runtime types of two objects (receiver and argument), unlike single dispatch which only considers the receiver's type

effective-polymorphism-hierarchy-biparti.text: The three major forms of polymorphism (ad hoc, parametric, subtyping) — each with distinct historical firsts (ALGOL 68, ML, Simula respectively) — would constitute a tripartite type-system expressiveness hierarchy with each form contributing fundamental capability, unless ad hoc polymorphism is merely syntactic sugar rather than a fundamental type-system feature, reducing the effective hierarchy to two fundamental dimensions (parametric and subtyping).

justifications.label: Ad hoc polymorphism's non-fundamental status reduces the effective polymorphism hierarchy from three to two

encapsulation-enforces-invariants-requir.text: Encapsulation's role in enforcing class invariants creates an asymmetry: the design principle is universal (applicable across paradigms), but its effectiveness depends entirely on language enforcement mechanisms — from C's opaque pointers to object-capability models — establishing invariant enforcement as the practical test of encapsulation quality rather than mere bundling or access restriction.

justifications.label: Invariant enforcement connects encapsulation's universal applicability to its language-specific ceiling, making enforcement the practical discriminator

encapsulation-gap-instantiates-theoretic.text: Encapsulation's bounded vertical stack — principle above, mechanism in the middle, with a theoretical ceiling (object-capabilities) that no language fully reaches and a practical floor (content coupling) below which design fails — may be a specific instance of a broader pattern where OO's theoretical ceilings relate to why pattern mechanisms are language-contingent: the ceilings establish fundamental bounds on what type systems can verify, and these bounds plausibly help explain why enforcement of principles like encapsulation and OCP remains permanently language-contingent, though the connection between the ceilings and the contingency has not been directly demonstrated.

justifications.label: Encapsulation's bounded stack is the same general phenomenon as OCP's language-contingent mechanisms, both hitting theoretical ceilings

encapsulation-is-cross-paradigm-module-p.text: Encapsulation applies beyond OOP (C opaque pointers, relational databases behind SQL, modules, ADTs, closures) and information hiding applies at every granularity level (classes through subsystems through hardware), establishing encapsulation as a universal architectural primitive that OO formalized but did not invent.

justifications.label: Cross-paradigm applicability plus multi-granularity information hiding establishes universality independent of OO

encapsulation-more-general-than-oop.text: Encapsulation is more general than OOP — for example, a relational database encapsulates behind SQL, hiding all internal machinery

encapsulation-not-information-hiding.text: Encapsulation and information hiding are related but distinct concepts: bundling (encapsulation) is structural, while hiding is about access control. Rogers (2001) explicitly argues this distinction.

encapsulation-not-oop-exclusive.text: Encapsulation is not unique to OOP — C's opaque pointer pattern, modules, abstract data types, libraries, and lexical closures all provide encapsulation. Existential types provide the theoretical foundation.

encapsulation-principle-mechanism-failur.text: Encapsulation operates at three independent architectural levels forming a complete vertical stack: principle level (information hiding segregates volatile decisions behind stable interfaces), mechanism level (access specifiers control usability independently of visibility), and module level (content coupling is the pathological failure mode when the stack is violated) — together establishing encapsulation's full path from abstract design goal to concrete failure diagnosis.

justifications.label: Three independent levels of encapsulation (principle, mechanism, failure) form complete vertical stack

encapsulation-required-to-enforce-invari.text: Encapsulation of state is required to enforce class invariants; some languages like Eiffel support class invariants enforced by the type system

encapsulation-sealed-by-object-capabilit.text: The principle-mechanism-pattern hierarchy (information hiding → encapsulation → Protected Variations) has a theoretical ceiling in the object-capability model, which uniquely guarantees that encapsulation cannot be bypassed — completing the chain from design principle to formal, unforgeable guarantee.

justifications.label: The information hiding chain reaches formal closure only through object capabilities, which seal encapsulation against reflection and ambient authority

encapsulation-stack-endures-as-module-fr.text: Encapsulation's complete vertical stack — information hiding (principle) above access control (mechanism) above Protected Variations (pattern), bounded by the object-capability ceiling and the content-coupling floor — persists beyond pattern dissolution because it addresses module-level concerns (segregating volatile decisions, controlling access to internals, protecting variation points) that are orthogonal to the type system, confirming encapsulation as a core component of the enduring module framework rather than a dissolving type-system artifact.

justifications.label: encapsulation's concerns are module-structural not type-systemic, so the dissolution fixed point predicts its persistence — and the vertical stack analysis confirms its completeness within the enduring framework

encapsulation-transcends-paradigm-bounda.text: Encapsulation is a cross-paradigm design concept with two distinct meanings (bundling and access restriction), applicable beyond OOP to modules, ADTs, databases, and lexical closures, and importantly distinct from information hiding — the design principle it mechanizes rather than synonymizes.

justifications.label: Three orthogonal clarifications establish encapsulation as paradigm-independent

encapsulation-two-meanings.text: Encapsulation has two distinct meanings: (1) a language mechanism for restricting direct access to an object's components (access control), and (2) a language construct that bundles data with the behavior operating on that data (cohesion).

encapsulation-universal-in-principle-lea.text: Encapsulation's universality across paradigms combined with information hiding's applicability at every granularity level together establish encapsulation as the most broadly applicable design principle — effective from class-level access control through subsystem boundaries to database abstraction layers.

justifications.label: Encapsulation is universal and scales to every granularity, unless all non-trivial abstractions inevitably leak (Spolsky's Law)

encapsulation-universal-with-language-sp.text: Encapsulation is simultaneously the most broadly applicable and the most language-sensitive OO concept: it applies across paradigms (OOP, modules, ADTs, relational databases) yet can only be fully guaranteed in languages supporting the object-capability model — universal in aspiration, language-specific in completeness.

justifications.label: Universal applicability combined with a language-specific completeness ceiling creates a unique tension in encapsulation's status

encapsulation-vertical-stack-has-theoret.text: Encapsulation operates as a complete vertical stack (principle → mechanism → pattern) that is bounded above by object-capabilities (theoretical ceiling) and below by content coupling (practical failure mode), establishing a fully characterized design space with known extremes.

justifications.label: The vertical stack and the capability ceiling together characterize encapsulation's full design space from failure mode to theoretical guarantee

enduring-module-framework-reliable-witho.text: The OO knowledge system's unified structure (single organizing axis, single intellectual genealogy) combined with the module framework's persistence beyond type-system dissolution together promise that OO's enduring intellectual contribution forms a coherent, reliable body of module design wisdom — but only if the module-level abstractions it prescribes (facades, cohesive interfaces, layered boundaries) don't leak, since Spolsky's law states all non-trivial abstractions are leaky.

justifications.label: Unified knowledge system + module framework persistence promise coherent enduring design wisdom — gated by whether prescribed abstractions leak in practice

enduring-semantic-contribution-limited-b.text: OO design's enduring semantic contributions — interface programming, composition preference, substitutability — deliver their promised design value only when encapsulation (the mechanism making these semantic contracts enforceable) is not systematically undermined by leaky abstractions that expose the implementation details the principles were designed to hide.

justifications.label: The enduring semantic contribution (depth-5) depends on encapsulation holding (depth-3), but Spolsky's law says all non-trivial abstractions leak — undermining enforcement of the very principles that endure

entire-gof-catalog-trends-toward-composi.text: The GoF catalog exhibits a consistent composition-over-inheritance trajectory across all three pattern categories: creational patterns internally mirror the composition theme (Abstract Factory and Prototype over Factory Method's subclassing), structural patterns use delegation as their shared wrapping mechanism, and behavioral patterns evolve from inheritance-based IoC (Template Method) to composition-based IoC (Strategy and DI).

justifications.label: Each GoF category independently demonstrates the composition preference — creational, structural, and behavioral all converge

epistemic-cycle-predictive-unless-abstra.text: The complete epistemic cycle (SoC → structured design → SOLID → GoF → dissolution → module framework) combined with the knowledge partition's double stability (structural overdetermination + dynamic self-correction) provides reliable predictive power for which OO concepts survive paradigm transitions, unless leaky abstractions — the universal property that no non-trivial abstraction perfectly hides its implementation — undermine the module boundaries on which the enduring framework's predictive stability depends.

justifications.label: Epistemic cycle predictions depend on module boundary integrity against abstraction leakage

error-driven-dissolution-ordering-confir.text: Error magnitude determines dissolution ordering at every organizational scale: within pattern families (creational self-healing mirrors catalog-scale dissolution, confirming fractal structure) and across families (creational-behavioral completeness asymmetry indexes error severity differences) — the same error-proportional mechanism operates at micro and macro scales.

justifications.label: fractal self-healing within families and error-magnitude asymmetry across families confirm scale-invariant error-driven ordering

error-magnitude-determines-dissolution-s.text: The error-proportional self-repair property (comprehensiveness of compensatory mechanisms indexing severity of the underlying type-system error) combined with the ordered dissolution sequence (mechanisms before governance) together suggest that more elaborate compensatory mechanisms tend to dissolve earlier in part because they compensate larger errors: mechanism-level compensations that index greater type-system deficiencies dissolve before simpler governance constraints that index smaller errors — dissolution sequence appears to be influenced by error magnitude, though the antecedents establish correlation and temporal ordering rather than a fully specified causal mechanism.

justifications.label: Error-proportional repair provides the WHY behind dissolution ordering: larger errors generate more elaborate compensations which dissolve first because they are most language-contingent

error-proportional-self-repair-feedback-.text: The OO knowledge system exhibits a pattern consistent with error-proportional self-repair: comprehensiveness appears to index the severity of the underlying type-system error (the four inheritance pathologies demanded correspondingly extensive compensatory infrastructure), while self-healing operates at multiple scales of that infrastructure (from individual pattern families to the full catalog). Together these suggest a relationship where greater error severity generates both more compensatory infrastructure and more organizational scales at which self-healing can operate.

justifications.label: error severity determines infrastructure volume (comprehensiveness), infrastructure volume determines self-healing surface area (fractal scales), together forming an error-proportional feedback loop

expression-problem-dissolves-with-type-s.text: The expression problem manifests in GoF patterns precisely because those patterns are type-system workarounds — in languages with sum types or multiple dispatch, both the Visitor/Interpreter patterns and the extensibility dilemma they embody dissolve simultaneously, revealing the expression problem in OO as a symptom of limited dispatch rather than an inherent design tension.

justifications.label: depth-3 — the expression problem and the patterns it inhabits share the same root cause (type-system limitations)

expression-problem-in-gof-patterns.text: The Visitor pattern's trade-off — easy to add operations, hard to add types — is the GoF manifestation of the expression problem, and the same structural rigidity constrains Interpreter to simple, stable grammars since both operate over Composite-based type hierarchies.

justifications.label: both patterns lock in the type dimension of a Composite structure, making new types expensive

expression-problem-is-extensibility-comp.text: The expression problem — structurally located at Composite (where Visitor adds operations easily but new types break all visitors) — is the completeness barrier for the GoF behavioral extensibility architecture: no single-dispatch type system can simultaneously support open addition of both types and operations, making behavioral extensibility necessarily incomplete within classical OO.

justifications.label: The expression problem's structural manifestation at Composite explains why behavioral extensibility is contingent on type system features

expression-problem-rooted-in-single-disp.text: The expression problem's manifestation in GoF patterns is structurally determined by single-dispatch limitations: Visitor's three-participant architecture exists specifically to simulate double dispatch, and both Visitor and Interpreter's extensibility trade-offs dissolve in languages with multiple dispatch or sum types, revealing the expression problem as a dispatch mechanism deficiency rather than an inherent design tension.

justifications.label: Visitor's dispatch architecture, its extensibility trade-off, and its dissolution all trace to single-dispatch as the structural root cause

extensibility-and-ioc-share-pattern-mech.text: Framework architecture (IoC via Template Method) and code extensibility (OCP via Strategy, Factory Method, Visitor, Decorator, Proxy) share at least one pattern mechanism — Factory Method, which combines with Template Method for IoC and independently serves as one of several mechanisms enabling OCP — suggesting that framework extension points and the Open-Closed Principle draw on overlapping pattern infrastructure.

justifications.label: Factory Method bridges IoC (via Template Method combination) and OCP (as a direct mechanism), unifying framework design and extensibility

extensibility-is-type-system-limitation-.text: OO extensibility concerns are fundamentally type system limitations rather than inherent design challenges: both the pattern mechanisms that realize OCP (Strategy, Visitor, Decorator, Proxy) and the expression problem that manifests through Composite/Visitor/Interpreter are language-contingent artifacts that simplify or dissolve with richer type features like multiple dispatch and sum types.

justifications.label: Two independent language-contingency chains (OCP mechanisms and expression problem) converge on the same conclusion: extensibility difficulty is a language deficiency, not a design universal.

facade-and-mediator-complementary-commun.text: Facade and Mediator both reduce communication complexity but in complementary directions: Facade provides unidirectional subsystem simplification (many internal classes hidden behind one interface for external clients), while Mediator enables multidirectional peer coordination (many colleagues communicate through one hub) — together they cover the full spectrum from hierarchical to cooperative communication.

justifications.label: Facade simplifies inward access, Mediator coordinates lateral interaction — complementary coverage of communication complexity

facade-embodies-lod-and-dip-at-subsystem.text: Facade can simultaneously instantiate LoD (subsystem-level Demeter enforcement) and DIP (clients depend on the Facade abstraction rather than subsystem implementation details, with the abstraction owned by the client layer) — making it a pattern where coupling reduction (LoD) and dependency direction (DIP) can converge at the subsystem boundary.

justifications.label: Facade uniquely satisfies both LoD and DIP at the subsystem level

facade-may-add-functionality.text: A Facade object may perform additional functionality before/after forwarding requests to subsystem classes — it is not limited to pure delegation.

facade-mediator-complete-communication-s.text: Facade and Mediator together provide architecturally complete communication simplification — Facade for unidirectional subsystem access (enforcing LoD at the boundary) and Mediator for multidirectional peer coordination — covering both the client-to-subsystem and peer-to-peer communication patterns.

justifications.label: Facade handles the easy case (unidirectional, LoD-aligned), but the Mediator half of the simplification is fragile — it risks becoming the god object it was designed to prevent.

facade-pattern-gof-structural.text: The Facade pattern is a structural design pattern from the GoF catalog (Design Patterns, 1994, pp. 185ff) that provides a simplified, unified interface to a complex subsystem of classes.

facade-reduces-client-dependencies.text: The Facade pattern reduces client dependencies on subsystem classes: clients depend only on the Facade, replacing tight coupling with loose coupling.

facade-vs-adapter-vs-decorator.text: Facade, Adapter, and Decorator serve distinct purposes: Adapter converts one interface to match what a client expects, Decorator dynamically adds/alters behavior at runtime, and Facade provides a simplified interface for complexity reduction.

factory-method-abstract-or-default.text: A factory method can be abstract (forcing subclasses to implement it) or concrete with a default implementation (allowing optional override).

factory-method-combines-with-template-me.text: Factory Method frequently combines with Template Method: the creator's constructor defines the algorithm skeleton while the factory method is the hook that varies the concrete product type (as shown in the GoF MazeGame example).

factory-method-gof-creational.text: The Factory Method pattern is a creational GoF pattern whose intent is: 'Define an interface for creating an object, but let subclasses decide which class to instantiate. Factory method lets a class defer instantiation to subclasses.'

factory-method-is-class-creational.text: Factory Method is the only class-creational pattern among the GoF creational patterns; Abstract Factory, Builder, Prototype, and Singleton are object-creational

factory-method-reliable-extension-unless.text: Factory Method provides reliable product variation through inheritance-based extension points (subclassing the creator to vary the product), combining naturally with Template Method for framework architecture.

justifications.label: Factory Method's inheritance-based variation mechanism is reliable unless the fragile base class problem undermines inheritance stability

factory-method-relies-on-inheritance.text: The Factory Method pattern relies on inheritance (subclassing) to vary the product being created, distinguishing it from Abstract Factory which uses object composition.

factory-method-requires-subclassing-not-.text: Factory Method requires subclassing the creator to vary products but does not require an initialization operation; Prototype is the inverse — no subclassing but requires initialize()

factory-method-supports-open-closed-prin.text: Factory Method supports the Open-Closed Principle: new product types can be added by creating new ConcreteCreator subclasses without modifying existing creator code.

final-sealed-and-non-leaf-abstract-compl.text: Marking classes final/sealed (preventing further subclassing to enable static dispatch) and making non-leaf classes abstract (ensuring all concrete classes are leaves to prevent fragile base class issues) are complementary constraints that together define a complete hierarchy discipline: non-leaf-abstract determines WHERE concrete classes may exist (only at leaves), while final/sealed ensures they REMAIN leaves (preventing further extension that would reintroduce fragility).

justifications.label: Two complementary constraints partition hierarchy positions — one forces concreteness to leaves, the other locks leaves against further extension

final-sealed-enables-static-dispatch.text: Marking a class final (Java/C++) or sealed (C#) prevents subclassing and enables static dispatch (early binding) by eliminating vtable lookups

fixed-point-intellectual-foundation-prac.text: Data abstraction's theoretical foundation and SOLID's governance spine constitute the dissolution fixed point's intellectual foundation — providing both the "why" (separation of interface from implementation) and the "how" (module quality constraints) — unless composition's primary practical drawback (forwarding boilerplate) makes the governance framework impractical to implement at scale.

justifications.label: fixed-point intellectual foundation depends on composition being practical despite forwarding cost

fixed-point-self-sustaining-via-complete.text: At the dissolution fixed point, GRASP and SOLID jointly provide comprehensive practical governance — GRASP addressing responsibility assignment (who creates, who knows, who coordinates) and SOLID addressing dependency structure (dependency direction, interface granularity, cohesion constraints) — while data abstraction provides the enduring theoretical foundation for interface programming. Together these constitute the fixed point's intellectual foundation, establishing both how dependencies should be governed and what grounds the module framework's durability.

justifications.label: practical governance (GRASP+SOLID) and theoretical foundation (data abstraction) jointly make the fixed point self-sustaining

flyweight-and-prototype-span-identity-de.text: Flyweight and Prototype represent opposite poles of the GoF's object identity design space: Flyweight maximizes sharing via value-semantic equality (many references, few objects), while Prototype maximizes independence via cloning (each reference, a distinct object) — together spanning from maximal sharing to maximal individuation.

justifications.label: Opposite identity models — sharing vs cloning — that together cover the full spectrum of object identity strategies

flyweight-bridges-object-and-value-seman.text: The Flyweight pattern uniquely bridges object and value semantics within the GoF catalog: intrinsic state creates shared identity (value-like equality and immutability), extrinsic state maintains per-use context (object-like individuality), and this split blurs the boundary between per-instance and shared class state.

justifications.label: Flyweight's state partitioning plus immutability requirement creates a hybrid object/value entity unique among GoF patterns

flyweight-factory-checks-existing.text: The FlyweightFactory must check for existing instances before creating new ones — this is the core sharing mechanism. The factory is often implemented as a Singleton.

flyweight-immutable-value-objects.text: Making flyweights into immutable value objects enables safe sharing across threads — two instances are equal if their values are equal.

flyweight-intrinsic-vs-extrinsic-state.text: In the Flyweight pattern, intrinsic state is invariant, context-independent, and stored inside the flyweight object; extrinsic state is variant, context-dependent, and passed in by the client at use time.

flyweight-pattern-gof-structural.text: The Flyweight pattern is a structural GoF design pattern that minimizes memory usage by sharing common data across many similar objects, separating intrinsic state (shared, context-independent) from extrinsic state (unique, context-dependent).

flyweight-pattern-space-optimization-sha.text: The Flyweight pattern achieves space optimization by sharing intrinsic (common) state across many objects while keeping extrinsic (context-specific) state external

flyweight-term-origin.text: The term 'Flyweight' was coined by Paul Calder and Mark Linton in 1990 for handling glyphs in a WYSIWYG editor, though similar techniques existed as early as 1988 in the ET++ framework.

four-pattern-categories-creational-struc.text: The four design pattern categories are Creational (object creation), Structural (class/object composition), Behavioral (object collaboration), and Concurrency (concurrent processing)

four-wrapper-patterns-taxonomy.text: Adapter, Decorator, Facade, and Proxy are structural wrapping patterns commonly distinguished by intent: interface conversion, behavior addition, interface simplification, and access control respectively. However, intent alone may not fully distinguish them — for example, Proxy also typically manages the lifecycle of the wrapped object, a distinction not captured by access control alone.

justifications.label: structurally similar patterns differentiated by purpose, not mechanism

fragile-base-class-problem.text: The fragile base class problem — modifications to a base class causing unintended behavioral changes in subclasses — is the central criticism of implementation inheritance

fragile-base-class-strengthens-compositi.text: The fragile base class problem provides a fourth independent line of evidence for composition-over-inheritance, beyond the three already identified (theoretical via LSP, empirical via modern languages, mechanistic via delegation): implementation fragility — where base class modifications cause unintended subclass breakage — directly demonstrates inheritance's maintenance cost in practice.

justifications.label: Fragile base class adds a practical-maintenance argument independent of the three existing theoretical/empirical/mechanistic lines

function-overloading-static-cannot-achie.text: Function overloading resolves on the static (declared) type of the argument at compile time, so it alone cannot achieve double dispatch which requires runtime type resolution

go-omits-inheritance-favors-composition.text: Go deliberately omits inheritance, favoring composition ('has-a' over 'is-a').

go-rust-composition-only-no-inheritance.text: Go and Rust use type composition exclusively, having no class-based inheritance at all.

god-object-antipattern.text: God Object is an anti-pattern in OOP describing an object that knows or does too much.

god-object-is-canonical-anti-cohesion-ex.text: The God Object anti-pattern is the canonical anti-cohesion exemplar: it simultaneously violates SRP's actor-based formulation (responsible to all actors rather than one) and Constantine's cohesion measure (elements within it do not belong together), establishing the God Object as the negative archetype against which module governance is defined.

justifications.label: God Object violates both SRP's actor constraint and cohesion's belonging measure, making it the canonical anti-pattern

god-object-is-srp-violation-at-coordinat.text: The God Object anti-pattern and Mediator's god-object tradeoff are manifestations of SRP violation at different architectural levels: God Object violates one-actor responsibility at the class level, while Mediator risks the same violation at the coordination level as centralized interaction logic accumulates responsibilities to multiple actors.

justifications.label: God Object and Mediator bloat are the same SRP violation (multiple actors) manifesting at class vs coordination scale

gof-23-patterns-5-7-11.text: The Gang of Four (1994) cataloged 23 design patterns: 5 Creational, 7 Structural, and 11 Behavioral.

gof-authors-gamma-helm-johnson-vlissides.text: The Gang of Four (GoF) authors are Gamma, Helm, Johnson, and Vlissides, who published Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software in 1994

gof-authors-validate-pattern-contingency.text: The GoF authors themselves validate that design patterns are historically contingent: their 2009 retrospective would add patterns (DI, extension object) and remove others (Singleton), confirming Norvig's independent finding that patterns evolve and dissolve as language features mature.

justifications.label: Both the catalog authors and independent researchers converge on patterns being contingent rather than universal

gof-book-23-patterns-three-categories.text: The GoF book Design Patterns (1994) defines exactly 23 patterns in 3 categories: 5 creational, 7 structural, 11 behavioral

gof-book-cpp-and-smalltalk-examples.text: The GoF Design Patterns book uses C++ and Smalltalk for its code examples

gof-catalog-both-self-conscious-and-self.text: The GoF catalog exhibits two reflexive properties simultaneously: self-conscious contingency (encoding its own evolution trajectory via Singleton→DI, Template Method→Strategy) and self-healing (the creational family containing and resolving its own Singleton pathology), making the catalog not merely a passive artifact but an actively self-correcting system.

justifications.label: Self-consciousness and self-healing are complementary reflexive properties: the catalog both diagnoses and treats its own internal pathologies

gof-catalog-contains-seeds-of-own-evolut.text: The GoF catalog encodes within itself the trajectory of its own evolution: creational patterns evolve from Singleton to DI, behavioral patterns evolve from inheritance-based IoC (Template Method) to composition-based IoC (Strategy/DI), and both trajectories independently converge on the catalog's own composition-over-inheritance recommendation — making the book a snapshot of a transition it was already accelerating.

justifications.label: Two independent evolution arcs within the catalog (creational and behavioral) both point away from inheritance toward composition, revealing the catalog as transitional rather than terminal

gof-catalog-previews-own-dissolution-thr.text: The GoF catalog is not merely self-consciously contingent (acknowledging through retrospective and Norvig's analysis that patterns evolve with languages) — it concretely previews its own dissolution trajectory through MVC, whose notification mechanism evolves from synchronous Observer to asynchronous pub/sub and whose control mechanism evolves from inheritance-based Template Method to composition-based Strategy, demonstrating in a single compound pattern exactly how the catalog dissolves.

justifications.label: Abstract self-awareness of contingency plus a concrete working example of dissolution together demonstrate the catalog contains its own succession narrative

gof-catalog-self-consciously-contingent.text: The GoF catalog is self-consciously contingent: it internally encodes its own evolution trajectory (Singleton→DI, Template Method→Strategy, Visitor→multiple dispatch) and its authors retroactively acknowledged patterns as historically situated artifacts that would be revised with hindsight.

justifications.label: The catalog's internal evolution signals and its authors' 2009 retrospective jointly demonstrate awareness of contingency from both within and without

gof-dissolution-follows-own-organizing-a.text: The GoF catalog dissolves along its own organizing axis: as languages internalize composition-over-inheritance (the principle around which the entire catalog is organized), the patterns that implement composition mechanisms become unnecessary — the catalog succeeds by making itself obsolete.

justifications.label: Languages absorb the catalog's central insight, dissolving the patterns that taught it

gof-eleven-behavioral-patterns.text: The Gang of Four defined 11 behavioral patterns: Chain of Responsibility, Command, Interpreter, Iterator, Mediator, Memento, Observer, State, Strategy, Template Method, and Visitor

gof-favor-composition-not-eliminate-inhe.text: The GoF Design Patterns book (1994) recommends 'Favor object composition over class inheritance' but does not say to eliminate inheritance entirely — they note the two typically work hand-in-hand.

gof-favored-composite-transparency.text: The original GoF book favors transparency over type safety for the Composite pattern, placing child-manipulation methods on the Component interface.

gof-five-creational-patterns.text: The GoF book defines exactly five canonical creational patterns: Abstract Factory, Builder, Factory Method, Prototype, and Singleton

gof-language-choice-demonstrates-pre-dis.text: The GoF book's use of C++ and Smalltalk — languages supporting inheritance as a primary mechanism but lacking sum types, multiple dispatch, and first-class functions — combined with its careful "favor composition, not eliminate inheritance" framing demonstrates the pre-dissolution state: patterns were formulated under specific type-system constraints that the dissolution trajectory subsequently relaxes.

justifications.label: Book's language choices and hedged framing capture the historical moment before dissolution became feasible

gof-patterns-are-language-contingent.text: GoF design patterns are language-contingent workarounds rather than universal design truths: they compensate for specific type system deficiencies and dissolve when those limitations are removed, as demonstrated by both multiple dispatch eliminating Visitor and first-class functions eliminating Command.

justifications.label: Two independent depth-1 conclusions — pattern dissolution by language feature and by type system richness — converge on a general thesis about the nature of design patterns

gof-principles-form-complete-grounded-gu.text: The GoF's two core principles, when grounded in their theoretical foundations, provide substantial guidance across complementary dimensions of dependency design: "program to an interface" (operationalized by the class/type distinction and validated by LSP's three-layer requirements) addresses WHAT to depend on, while "favor composition" (motivated by inheritance fragility and LSP violations) addresses HOW to structure those dependencies — together covering major dependency decisions.

justifications.label: The two principles achieve completeness by covering orthogonal dependency questions (target and structure) each with independent grounding

gof-principles-guarantee-system-correctn.text: The GoF's two core principles guarantee system correctness when fully implemented: "program to an interface" ensures behavioral substitutability via LSP's three layers, and "favor composition" ensures structural flexibility without inheritance fragility — together sufficient for provably correct OO systems.

justifications.label: The principles would guarantee correctness if LSP could be verified, but undecidability means they remain necessary-but-not-sufficient

gof-retrospective-add-remove-patterns.text: In a 2009 retrospective, the GoF authors would have added dependency injection, extension object, type object, and null object; Gamma wanted to remove Singleton but lacked consensus

gof-seven-structural-patterns.text: The GoF defines seven canonical structural patterns: Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade, Flyweight, and Proxy

gof-state-management-spans-three-design-.text: Flyweight, Prototype, and Memento collectively span at least two key design dimensions of GoF state management: identity (Flyweight's value-semantic sharing vs Prototype's unique cloning) and mutability/isolation strategy (Flyweight's immutable sharing vs Memento's deep-copied mutable snapshots). These patterns occupy distinct positions in this design space, covering a significant range of object state decisions from maximal sharing to maximal individuation and from share-by-immutability to copy-to-isolate.

justifications.label: Flyweight-Prototype span identity; Memento-Flyweight span mutability — combining these reveals a three-dimensional state management design space

gof-three-pattern-categories.text: The Gang of Four organized the 23 design patterns into three categories: Creational (object construction), Structural (object composition), and Behavioral (object communication)

gof-two-core-principles.text: The two core design principles from the GoF book are 'program to an interface, not an implementation' and 'favor object composition over class inheritance'

gof-two-principles-partition-oo-design-s.text: The GoF's two core principles address complementary aspects of OO design: "program to an interface" defines WHAT to depend on (operationalized through the class/type distinction, which enables polymorphic design and substitutability), while "favor composition over inheritance" defines HOW to structure relationships (motivated by inheritance breaking encapsulation and LSP violations, realized through delegation and pattern mechanisms) — together they provide substantial guidance for dependency design.

justifications.label: The two principles address orthogonal concerns (dependency target vs. dependency structure) that together cover the full design space

governance-and-pathology-co-dissolve-in-.text: SOLID's comprehensive module governance and inheritance's four independent pathologies dissolve together as languages evolve: SOLID exists partly to mitigate inheritance's problems (SRP prevents god classes, OCP channels extension safely, LSP constrains substitutability, DIP decouples dependency direction), so as languages move beyond class-based inheritance, both the problems and the governance apparatus designed to manage them become unnecessary — the patient and the treatment disappear simultaneously.

justifications.label: SOLID governance partly addresses inheritance pathologies; when languages transcend inheritance, both the problems and their governance solutions become moot

grasp-and-solid-independently-formalize-.text: GRASP and SOLID exhibit partial convergence on underlying design forces — Protected Variations and OCP share the information-hiding principle as a common foundation, while High Cohesion relates to both SRP and ISP as complementary cohesion constraints at different levels — suggesting these patterns address recurring structural concerns in modular design rather than being purely independent inventions.

justifications.label: depth-2 — two independent formalizations converging on the same forces constitutes stronger evidence than either alone

grasp-and-solid-jointly-govern-dissoluti.text: GRASP's responsibility assignment heuristics and SOLID's module governance spine both persist at the dissolution fixed point, addressing complementary concerns: SOLID governs module structure (dependency direction, interface granularity, cohesion constraints) while GRASP governs module interaction (who creates, who knows, who coordinates) — together they provide complete governance coverage for the enduring module framework.

justifications.label: GRASP + SOLID provide complementary fixed-point governance — structure and interaction

grasp-assignment-complements-solid-gover.text: GRASP's responsibility assignment heuristics (Information Expert assigns to the knowledge holder, Creator assigns to the lifecycle owner, Controller assigns to the system-event handler) and SOLID's quality governance (SRP/ISP constraining internal cohesion, DIP/OCP constraining external coupling) operate at complementary methodological levels: GRASP determines WHICH object receives a responsibility, while SOLID constrains HOW that responsibility is structured and interconnected — together forming a complete design methodology from initial responsibility assignment through quality governance.

justifications.label: GRASP assigns what SOLID constrains, covering the full design methodology

grasp-controller-beyond-ui.text: GRASP's Controller is the first object beyond the UI layer that handles system events — it does not do the work itself but delegates. It comes in two variants: use case controller and facade controller.

grasp-created-by-larman.text: GRASP was created by Craig Larman, first published in 'Applying UML and Patterns' (1997, 2nd ed. 2001, 3rd ed. 2004).

grasp-creator-four-criteria.text: GRASP's Creator pattern assigns class B the responsibility to create object A when B satisfies one or more criteria: contains/aggregates A, records A, closely uses A, or has A's initializing data.

grasp-extends-rediscovery-with-novel-bri.text: GRASP's Pure Fabrication — a non-domain class invented solely for module quality — addresses a tension between domain modeling and structural design that is not explicitly resolved within either Constantine's original structured design framework or SOLID's principle system. As an escape hatch that bridges domain-driven responsibility assignment with cohesion/coupling optimization, Pure Fabrication illustrates how independent formalization of recurring structural forces can surface concepts that go beyond parallel rediscovery, though whether this represents genuinely novel conceptual territory or an implicit consequence of the inherited framework applied in a new paradigm remains a matter of interpretation.

justifications.label: GRASP's Pure Fabrication is a novel concept that emerged from—not just restated by—rediscovery

grasp-information-expert-principle.text: GRASP's Information Expert principle states: assign responsibility to the class that has the information needed to fulfill it — look at what data is required, find where it lives, and place the method there.

grasp-low-coupling-high-cohesion-evaluat.text: GRASP's Low Coupling and High Cohesion are evaluative patterns (qualities to assess and aim for), not constructive patterns (specific structures to build).

grasp-nine-patterns.text: GRASP consists of exactly nine patterns: Information Expert, Creator, Controller, Indirection, Low Coupling, High Cohesion, Polymorphism, Protected Variations, and Pure Fabrication.

grasp-protected-variations-wraps-instabi.text: GRASP's Protected Variations pattern identifies points of predicted instability and wraps them with a stable interface, using polymorphism for varying implementations. It is closely related to the SOLID Open/Closed Principle.

grasp-pure-fabrication-bridges-domain-an.text: GRASP's Pure Fabrication — a class invented solely for low coupling, high cohesion, and reuse rather than domain modeling — bridges domain-driven responsibility assignment (Information Expert, Creator, Controller) with module quality optimization (cohesion/coupling), acknowledging that strict domain fidelity sometimes conflicts with module design quality and providing a principled escape hatch.

justifications.label: Pure Fabrication resolves the tension between domain-faithful responsibility assignment and module quality optimization within a single framework

grasp-pure-fabrication-not-domain-concep.text: GRASP's Pure Fabrication is a class that does not represent a domain concept, invented solely to achieve low coupling, high cohesion, and reuse. It corresponds to the 'service' concept in Domain-Driven Design.

grasp-pure-fabrication-validates-module-.text: GRASP's Pure Fabrication concept — classes invented for module quality rather than domain modeling — independently validates the module framework's orthogonality to the type system: Pure Fabrication exists precisely because module quality (cohesion, coupling) operates on a dimension independent of the domain type hierarchy.

justifications.label: Pure Fabrication is direct evidence that module quality operates on a dimension orthogonal to the type system

grasp-responsibility-covers-full-object-.text: GRASP provides three complementary responsibility assignment heuristics: Information Expert assigns responsibility to the class that has the information needed to fulfill it, Creator assigns instantiation responsibility to classes satisfying criteria like containment, aggregation, recording, close use, or possession of initializing data, and Controller designates the first object beyond the UI layer to handle and delegate system events.

justifications.label: Three heuristics covering behavior, creation, and coordination responsibilities without overlap

grasp-responsibility-persists-at-dissolu.text: GRASP's responsibility assignment heuristics — complementing SOLID's module governance at the object interaction level — address module-level coordination concerns (who creates, who knows, who controls) that are orthogonal to the type system, and therefore persist at the dissolution fixed point alongside the module framework they help organize.

justifications.label: GRASP addresses type-system-orthogonal concerns that endure at fixed point

hannemann-kiczales-aop-removed-dependenc.text: Hannemann and Kiczales showed that aspect-oriented programming (AspectJ) removed code-level dependencies from 17 of 23 GoF patterns

hierarchy-discipline-limited-by-fragile-.text: The combined hierarchy discipline — making non-leaf classes abstract and leaf classes final/sealed — successfully mitigates the inheritance-subtyping gap (ensuring concrete classes are leaf nodes with no subclass coupling, reducing LSP violation surface area) but remains vulnerable to the fragile base class problem, where base class implementation changes cascade to subclasses regardless of the hierarchy's abstract-leaf discipline.

justifications.label: Hierarchy discipline addresses substitutability (LSP) but not implementation fragility (fragile base class) — the two pathologies require different mitigations

hierarchy-discipline-mitigates-inheritan.text: The combined hierarchy discipline — making non-leaf classes abstract (ensuring all concrete classes are leaf nodes with no subclass coupling) and marking leaf classes final/sealed (preventing further subclassing to enable static dispatch) — directly mitigates the inheritance-subtyping gap that is the root cause of LSP violations: by forcing all concrete types to be leaf nodes, the discipline eliminates the scenario where a concrete superclass's implementation details create behavioral assumptions that subtypes violate.

justifications.label: the non-leaf-abstract + leaf-final discipline eliminates concrete superclasses, removing the primary site where inheritance-subtyping gap manifests as LSP violations

high-cohesion-does-not-mean-single-metho.text: High cohesion does not mean single-method classes — a single-function module has perfect cohesion but may be too narrow and creates tight coupling to other modules.

hohpe-eight-dimensions-of-coupling.text: Hohpe identifies eight dimensions of coupling: Technology, Location, Topology, Data Format & Type, Semantic, Conversation, Order, and Temporal dependency.

info-hiding-applies-at-every-granularity.text: Information hiding applies at every level of granularity — classes, modules, subsystems, databases, and hardware components — not just at the class level

info-hiding-hides-design-decisions-not-j.text: Information hiding is about hiding design decisions likely to change, not merely hiding data

info-hiding-is-principle-encapsulation-i.text: Information hiding is the design principle (segregate volatile decisions behind stable interfaces); encapsulation is the mechanism or technique that enforces it
